Description: If the material is sediments, include an additional field specifying the context from which the sediments were sampled—for example, human graves or stratigraphic units. Also, provide the relevant permitting authority (permits may differ based on context)

Since the table allows entering negative controls (‘negative control status’), there needs to be a way to show which samples are covered by a given negative control. One option could be a ‘Control Batch’ field to specify which samples are processed together with a given negative control (thats what I have in my project tables)
Term should be recommended as this can greatly facilitate and benefit future studies using the material and/or help identify sample distribution across studies.

Coordinates of the site or sample location, to be kept quite general (e.g. within a 20km radius or the nearest town) to avoid possibility of site looting. For instance, if the site features human burials, these are easily observable at ground level. So it would be better to keep the coordinates relatively broad but that are still representative of the ambient zone/area. Could also offer an option stating coordinates are not given due to sensitivity of the data.
Comment: Add to FAQ;: up to submitter how precise you can be with your coordinates; Exists: `lat_lon`

Description: He would like a secondary column for the location the sample was stored in for the longest time and not just at the moment. Or at least expand the examples, because he thinks it's misleading.

Duration of the sample storage location', to be associated with 'samp_store_loc'. This essentially keeps track of when a sample was stored in a specific freezer, drawer etc... There can be multiple storage locations for a sample over time, which would require different durations/dates.
Comment: Very unlikely people record this information regularly, and limited use

Description: The term refers to the general conditions under which samples taken for nucleic acid analysis are stored prior to extraction, particularly in cases of long-term storage that may influence nucleic acid recovery or library construction (for example, storage at room temperature or frozen).  One example are soil cores sometimes stored for years in a freezer prior to DNA extraction.

new tabs proposed for dating: individuals__radio_14Cdate, to be changed to individuals__raw_radio_14Cdate: The raw C14 value sent by the dating company, raw, untouched, uncalibrated. In other words, the value given by the C14-dating company.

new tabs proposed for dating: individuals__radio_14Cerror, to be changed to individuals__raw_radio_14Cerror: 
The error of the raw C14 value sent by the dating company, raw, untouched, uncalibrated.

new tabs proposed for dating: individuals__radio_14Clabcode: 
The code of the measurement provided by the dating company (i.e., this is a unique code, with the following format : AA-90288 / UGA-8660 / D-AMS 030191 / CIRAM-5979).

new tabs proposed for dating: individuals__date_median to be renamed as individuals__calBP_C14_median_date: 
This is the calibrated median probability calculated by a calibration software on the raw C14 date of the individual. This value can be used in the analyses, and must be the same value that is included in the column individuals__date.

new tabs proposed for dating: Contextual dates of sites based of some individuals: 
– sites__radio_14C_date, changed to sites__radio_raw_average_14C_date: 
Average of all ABSOLUTE (Raw) and UNCALIBRATED dates available for that site that can be confidently associated with any other individual of the same site. Only archaeologists can provide the information of when this is possible to do. ATTENTION: is it not always the case that individuals of the same sites are synchronous. Very often archaeological sites are diachronic or chronologically asynchronous.
new tabs proposed for dating: sites__radio_14C_error to be renamed as sites__radio_raw_average_14C_error

new tabs proposed for dating: sites__calBP14C_median_date: 
This is the calibrated median probability calculated by a calibration software on the average of the uncalibrated C14 dates of the site. This value can be used in the analyses when there is no direct date of the individual (i.e., no value in individuals__calBP_C14_median_date), and in such cases, it must be the same value that is included in the column individuals__date.

new tabs proposed for dating: – individuals__contextual_date: 
When there is not direct date for the individual, nor association date for the site, this column will have any information of the individual’s date that is not derived from a direct or indirect radio C14 date (e.g., date mentioned in a historical record, archaeological information, dates inferred from objects, dates mentioned in a crypt, or just a very vast category, such as, pre-contact, Holocene, etc.).

new tabs proposed for dating: – individuals__date: 
This the column that contains the best information we can give from an individuals date (In order of relevance, this columns will include: individuals__calBP_C14_median_date -> sites__calBP14C_median_date –> individuals__contextual_date)

new tabs proposed for dating: – individuals__datation_method: 
This categoy is defined by precise, fixed categories (Individual_C14, Site_C14, Contextual, Historical record, objects, etc.)
